stories.title: SGA Playoff Career-High 42 — Thunder Crush Suns 121-109, Lead Series 3-0

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
SGA. 42 POINTS. 15-OF-18 FROM THE FIELD.

That's not just a playoff career high — it's one of the most efficient 40-point games in NBA playoff history. Thunder win 121-109. Suns face elimination.

OKC is rolling.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
42 pts. 15/18 FG. 11/12 FT. 8 ast.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander just joined TERRY PORTER as the only players to score 40+ in the playoffs while shooting 83%+ from the field.

Thunder lead 3-0. This is an MVP performance.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander put on the kind of performance that defines a player's legend. The Thunder guard finished with a playoff career-high 42 points Saturday afternoon, shooting 15-of-18 from the field and 11-of-12 from the free throw line to go with eight assists, as Oklahoma City demolished the Phoenix Suns 121-109 and took a commanding 3-0 series lead.

The efficiency alone made history. SGA became only the second player in NBA playoff history to score 40 or more points while shooting at least 83.3 percent from the field — joining Terry Porter. It wasn't just an individual performance; it was a systematic demolition. The Thunder, already playing without Jalen Williams (left hamstring), did not need him against a Suns team that had no answer for SGA's relentless pressure.

Phoenix now faces elimination in Game 4. No team in NBA history has come back from a 3-0 deficit to win a playoff series. The Suns' offseason — and perhaps the futures of Kevin Durant's former teammates — now hinges on a game they need but statistically cannot win.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: SGA Scores Playoff Career-High 42 as Thunder Rout Suns 121-109, Take 3-0 Series Lead

stories.title: Dosunmu 43 Points — Wolves Beat Nuggets 112-96 (G4), Lead 3-1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
AYO DOSUNMU. 43 POINTS. OFF THE BENCH.

13-of-17 from the field. 5-of-5 from 3. 12-of-12 from the line.

That's the best reserve scoring game in the NBA playoffs in 50 YEARS. Wolves win 112-96. Denver is on the brink.

#HoopHeroes #Wolves #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Edwards hurt. DiVincenzo hurt. Minnesota needed someone.

Ayo Dosunmu answered with 43 POINTS off the bench — the best reserve playoff performance since 1976.

Nuggets are down 3-1. Jokic and Denver are in serious trouble.

#HoopHeroes #Wolves #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

Ayo Dosunmu was not supposed to be the hero. With Anthony Edwards sidelined by a knee injury and Donte DiVincenzo also unavailable, the Minnesota Timberwolves needed someone to step into the void against the defending champion Denver Nuggets in Game 4. What they got was one of the most remarkable reserve performances in playoff history.

Dosunmu scored 43 points off the bench — shooting 13-of-17 from the field, going a perfect 5-of-5 from beyond the arc and 12-of-12 from the free throw line in 42 minutes. According to NBA records, it is the highest-scoring playoff game by a reserve in 50 years, since Fred Brown poured in 45 for Seattle against Phoenix on April 15, 1976. Minnesota won 112-96 and now leads 3-1, placing Denver one loss from an early exit.

Naz Reid supported with 17 points and 9 rebounds, but this was Dosunmu's night. The Nuggets, who have leaned on Nikola Jokic all season, could not manufacture enough offense to keep pace. Denver must now win three straight — starting with Game 5 — to stay alive.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Dosunmu Scores 43 Off the Bench — Best Reserve Playoff Performance in 50 Years — as Wolves Beat Nuggets 112-96

stories.title: KAT's Triple-Double Leads Knicks Past Hawks 114-98, Series Tied 2-2

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
KAT with a TRIPLE-DOUBLE in Atlanta.

20 pts. 10 reb. 10 ast. Anunoby added 22/10. Knicks won 114-98 and evened the series at 2-2.

New York went to Atlanta after a gut-punch Game 3 loss and answered with force.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Series tied 2-2. KAT triple-double. Anunoby double-double.

The Knicks showed up in Atlanta and dominated. 114-98. No drama this time — just straight physicality and ball movement.

Game 5 is coming. This series is locked in.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

The New York Knicks arrived in Atlanta with a chip on their shoulder and left with the series tied. After absorbing a gut-punch Game 3 loss to C.J. McCollum's game-winner, New York responded with authority — winning 114-98 behind Karl-Anthony Towns' triple-double and OG Anunoby's dominant two-way performance.

Towns finished with 20 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists, anchoring both ends for a Knicks team that imposed its physical style on the Hawks from the opening tip. Anunoby added 22 points and 10 rebounds, giving New York two genuine frontcourt weapons that Atlanta struggled to contain. The Knicks' defense was the story — stifling the same offense that had produced McCollum's miracle shot the game before.

The series is now tied 2-2 heading back to New York for Game 5. The Knicks have restored home court and momentum. After losing that Game 3 on a wild shot, New York made sure Game 4 would be decided long before the final seconds.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: KAT's Triple-Double Carries Knicks to 114-98 Win in Atlanta, Evens Series 2-2

stories.title: Magic Outlast Pistons 113-105, Take 2-1 Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Banchero 25. Bane 25. Magic hold on, 113-105.

Orlando outfought Detroit in the final minutes to take a 2-1 series lead. Two young teams. One is starting to pull ahead.

Game 4 up next for the Pistons.

#HoopHeroes #Magic #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:30 PM ET

The Orlando Magic passed their latest test. On Saturday morning, Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane each scored 25 points as Orlando held off the Detroit Pistons 113-105 in Game 3, giving the Magic a 2-1 series lead and a moment of validation for a team that has been battle-tested all season.

The game was close down the stretch, and Detroit pushed hard to take the series lead. But the Magic made the plays they needed to when it mattered most. Banchero, the 2022 No. 1 overall pick, continues to grow into his role as a playoff performer. Bane's 25 points off the floor alongside him gave Orlando two reliable threats that the Pistons couldn't shut down.

Cade Cunningham and the Pistons still have the firepower to get back in this series. Detroit won Game 2 and has not been blown out. But Orlando now has the advantage, and Game 4 gives the Magic a chance to go up 3-1 and put this series away.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Banchero and Bane Each Score 25 as Magic Hold Off Pistons 113-105, Take 2-1 Series Lead

stories.title: Sunday's Playoff Slate — Lakers Can Sweep, Celtics/Spurs/Cavs in Action

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
SUNDAY NBA PLAYOFF SCHEDULE

1 PM – Cavs at Raptors G4 (CLE 2-1) ESPN
3:30 PM – Spurs at Blazers G4 (SA 2-1) ESPN
7 PM – Celtics at 76ers G4 (BOS 2-1) NBC
9:30 PM – Lakers at Rockets G4 (LAL 3-0 — SWEEP CHANCE) NBC

LeBron could close it out tonight.

#HoopHeroes #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

Sunday's NBA playoff schedule has everything: a possible sweep, three series hanging in the balance, and Wembanyama's potential return looming over one of the most-watched games of the first round.

The afternoon tips off with Cleveland visiting Toronto at 1 p.m. ET (ESPN) with the Cavaliers holding a 2-1 lead. Then at 3:30 p.m. ET, the Spurs and Blazers meet in Portland for Game 4 with San Antonio ahead 2-1 — and Victor Wembanyama's availability still uncertain after his concussion protocol stint. The Boston Celtics look to extend their 2-1 advantage over Philadelphia at 7 p.m. ET on NBC.

The night closer: LeBron James and the Lakers can complete a first-round sweep of the Houston Rockets at 9:30 p.m. ET on NBC. Los Angeles leads 3-0, and LeBron hit a tying 3-pointer with 13 seconds left in regulation of Game 3 to force overtime. A sweep would give the Lakers an extra week of rest before the second round and send a message to the rest of the West.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Sunday NBA Playoffs Preview: Lakers Can Sweep, Three Series at Pivotal Moments
